content.problemStatement:
Chef owns a swimming pool with width NNN meters which contains NNN continuous walls with height hih_ihi​ (possible zero) and width 111 meter each.

When it rains heavily, water is stored in the swimming pool in the form of sections (check Figure 1). Chef is busy calling his friends for a party. So, you need to determine the maximum water stored in **a section** among all the sections after a heavy rain.

Figure 1 :



This swimming pool has 3 sections :


Section 2 has the maximum water i.e 10 units.
Section 1 has 9 units of water.
Section 3 contains 2 units of water.
So, the answer would be 10 units (maximum of all the sections)


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
First line of each testcase contains an integer NNN - number of walls AAA.
Second line contains N space separated integers h1,h2,.....,hNh_1, h_2, ....., h_Nh1​,h2​,.....,hN​


Output Format
For each testcase, output one integer in a single line - Maximum water stored among all sections.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains an integer N, the number of walls. The second line contains N space‑separated integers h1, h2, …, hN denoting the heights of the walls.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one integer – the maximum amount of water stored in any contiguous section after the rain.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: water amounts are [0,5,3,0]; one section with total 8.\nTest case 2: water amounts are [0,2,0,4,1,0]; two sections with totals 2 and 5, maximum is 5.

hidden.label: edge case with multiple tiny sections

solution.approach: For each test case we first compute for every position i the highest wall on its left (including i) – leftMax[i] – and the highest wall on its right – rightMax[i]. The water that can stay above position i is max(0, min(leftMax[i], rightMax[i]) - h[i]). This gives an array water[i]. A "section" is a maximal contiguous sub‑array where water[i] > 0. We scan water[] once, accumulating the sum of the current section and resetting the accumulator when water[i] becomes 0. The maximum accumulator value encountered is the answer. The algorithm runs in O(N) time and O(N) extra space (the two auxiliary max arrays).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the number of walls in that test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) auxiliary space for leftMax and rightMax arrays (can be reduced to O(1) with two‑pointer technique, but O(N) easily fits the constraints).
